utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so sophia i see that you you hurt your knee tell me about what happened

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i was jumping on my kid's trampoline and i could just slipped out from under me

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: my gosh one of those big trampolines in your back yard

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah a pretty big one

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay which knee was it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my right knee

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right knee okay and when did this happen

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: about four days ago

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great the weather was perfect this weekend so i'm glad you at least got outside sorry to hear you got hurt okay so your right knee did you did you feel it like pop or or snap or anything when you hurt it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i felt a little pop and then it swelled up really big afterward

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay did you try anything for the pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i took some ibuprofen and i put some ice on it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay did that help

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little bit but it's still really hard to get around

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright and have you have you been able to stand on it or does that hurt too much

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it hurts quite a bit to stand but i am able to put weight on it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and what part of the knee is it inside outside middle

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: kind of that inside part of my kneecap

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and okay so as long as you're here and then your primary care physician i'm looking through your chart and it looks like we're treating your diabetes so how you've been doing with your your diet overall are you are you keeping your sugars low

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's going okay i i forget to check quite a bit though

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: on it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i understand how has your diet been lately

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's been pretty good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay good good you know it's hard to stay away from the sugary foods sometimes i i enjoy ice cream regularly okay so let's do physical exam as long as you are here so i'm just gon na listen to your heart your heart sounds normal no murmurs or gallops listen to your lungs quick if you can take a deep breath lungs are clear that's good news let's take a look at that knee right knee looks like it definitely has some swelling i'm gon na do some maneuvers here does it hurt when i push you on the inside of the knee

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that hurts

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay how about the outside

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little bit but not as much

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so some pain on palpation on the inside little bit of pain on the outside of the knee if i bend the knee back does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how about when i extend it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that hurts

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so little bit of limited range of motion as well as pain on both flexion and extension on the knee i'm gon na push on this a little bit looks like your mcmurray's test is negative just checking for a meniscus tear okay so let's talk a little bit about your plan what i am concerned about for your knee is it sounds like you have a torn or injured mcl i it's that inside tendon in your knee so i'm concerned about that since you're having trouble with weightbearing and you heard that pop so what i'm gon na do is i'm gon na put you in a straight leg brace and i'll prescribe some mobic you can start taking that as a a pain reliever and to try to get some of the swelling down i want you to ice your knee once an hour for about fifteen minutes but i'm also gon na send you out for an mri because we wan na make sure this is what happens see if there's any other damage to the knee does that sound good

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that sounds great thank you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah and then for your diabetes as long as you're here it sounds like you're managing that pretty well but i do wan na get a recheck on your hemoglobin a1c and then i'm also i'm going to get a refill on the metformin that you have been taking five hundred milligrams so you can keep taking that as well so do you have any other questions for me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no that's it thanks

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright well thank you hope that you feel better

detail_list.summary: Right knee pain
The patient injured her knee while jumping on a trampoline.
The patient identifies the right knee as the site of injury.
The injury occurred four days ago.
The doctor asks if the patient felt a pop or snap during the injury.
The patient reports hearing a pop and experiencing significant swelling.
The doctor asks what the patient has tried for pain relief.
The patient used ibuprofen and ice for pain.
The doctor asks if the patient is able to stand or bear weight on the knee.
The patient reports pain with standing but is able to bear weight.
The doctor asks for the specific location of the knee pain.
The patient reports pain on the medial aspect of the knee.
Diabetes
The doctor asks about the patient's diet and blood sugar management.
The patient reports forgetting to check blood sugars frequently.
The doctor asks about the patient's recent diet.
The patient reports her diet has been good.
The doctor performs a physical exam, noting clear lungs, no murmurs or gallops, right knee swelling, and pain on palpation of the medial and lateral knee.
The doctor asks if bending the knee causes pain.
The patient reports pain with knee flexion.
The doctor asks if extending the knee causes pain.
The patient reports pain with knee extension.
The doctor notes limited range of motion, pain on flexion and extension, and a negative McMurray's test.
The doctor plans to provide a straight leg brace, prescribe Mobic, instruct on icing the knee hourly for 15 minutes, and order an MRI.
The doctor orders an A1c test and a refill for metformin 500 mg.
